info.title: Gen2X API Reference
info.description:
﻿Use this API to configure and control Impinj Gen2X features on Zebra fixed RFID readers over MQTT.

## Overview

This tutorial provides a walk-through of the steps to use Impinj Gen2X tag features via the MQTT API on Zebra fixed RFID readers, including:

- **Tag Protection** — Lock individual tags with a password so they're invisible to unauthorized readers.
- **FastID** — Return the EPC and TID in a single inventory response.
- **TagFocus** — Silence already-read tags so the reader focuses on new ones.
- **Tag Quieting** — Silence specific tags by EPC ID.

Impinj Gen2X extends the Gen2 radio and logical layers. Your tags must support Gen2X to use these features. To check whether your tags are compatible, see the [Impinj Gen2X tag specifications](http://www.impinj.com/Gen2X).

## Before you begin

Set up your MQTT connection, broker, and topic before using this API. For setup instructions, see the [Zebra IoTC MQTT Setup Guide](https://zebradevs.github.io/rfid-ziotc-docs/other_cloud_support/MQTT/index.html).

## Get started
Follow these steps to configure and apply Gen2X features on the reader:

1. **Check current state (optional)** — Send `get_impinjGen2X` to view the currently active Gen2X configuration.
2. **Stop the radio** — Send the `stop` command. The radio must be stopped before applying Gen2X configuration changes.
3. **Configure features** — Send `set_impinjGen2X` to stage the Gen2X features you want to enable (for example, TagProtect, FastID, and TagFocus).
4. **Verify staged configuration** — Send `get_impinjGen2X` to confirm the staged values are correct.
5. **Start with Gen2X** — Send the `start` command with `applyImpinjGen2X` set to `true` to apply the staged configuration and start the radio.
6. **Confirm applied state** — Send `get_impinjGen2X` again to verify the configuration is active.

tags.name: Tag Protection
tags.description:
﻿Use TagProtect to lock individual tags with a 32-bit password. A protected tag is invisible to all readers until you provide the correct password, preventing unauthorized reading and cloning.

Use the commands in this section to enable or disable protection on a tag, configure reader visibility, and enable short-range mode.

tags.name: FastID
tags.description:
﻿Use FastID to receive both the EPC and TID (Tag Identifier) in a single inventory response. This eliminates the need for a separate TID read operation and improves inventory throughput.

Use the commands in this section to enable or disable FastID on the reader.

tags.name: TagFocus
tags.description:
﻿Use TagFocus to improve read rates in dense tag environments. After a tag is inventoried, it stays silent in subsequent rounds so the reader can focus on new or unread tags. TagFocus targets tags in session S1.

Use the commands in this section to enable or disable TagFocus on the reader.

tags.name: Tag Quieting
tags.description:
﻿Use Tag Quieting to silence specific tags by EPC ID. Quieted tags do not respond during inventory rounds, reducing unwanted reads and improving accuracy in high-density environments.

Use the commands in this section to quiet and unquiet individual tags.

tags.name: Gen2X Configuration Retrieval
tags.description: ﻿Use the configuration commands to read back the Gen2X settings that are currently stored on the reader. This lets you verify which features — such as TagProtect, FastID, TagFocus, and Tag Quieting — are active before you start the radio.

tags.name: Control Commands
tags.description: Start and stop the radio while applying Gen2X configuration.

post.description:
﻿Locks a specific tag so it becomes invisible to all readers that don't have the correct password.

Provide the tag's EPC ID and 32-bit access password. After you send this command, the tag stops responding to standard inventory and doesn't respond to Gen2 commands unless the reader provides the correct password.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Enables Tag Protection on the tag

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

post.description:
﻿Removes the TagProtect lock from a specific tag. After you send this command, the tag becomes visible to all readers without requiring a password.

Provide the same EPC ID and password that you used when you enabled protection.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Disables Tag Protection on the tag

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

post.description:
﻿Grants the reader permission to see protected tags during inventory. Provide the correct access password and the reader uses it to unmask protected tags in inventory rounds.

You don't need to specify individual tag IDs. All protected tags that match the password become visible to the reader.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Enables the reader to read protected tags

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Enable the reader to see protected tags

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string

post.description: ﻿Revokes the reader's permission to see protected tags. After you send this command, the reader no longer surfaces protected tags in inventory results, even if a password was previously configured.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Revoke the reader's ability to see protected tags

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string

post.description:
﻿Protects a specific tag and restricts it to respond only when it's physically close to the antenna (short-range mode).

This is useful for precise picking, point-of-sale, or any scenario where you need to ensure only the nearest tag is read. Provide the tag EPC ID, the 32-bit access password, and set `enableShortRange` to `true`.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

action.description: Sets the tag to protected mode

tagID.description: Hexadecimal tagID (EPC) of the target tag

enableShortRange.description: true to enable short-range protection mode for higher security

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Protect a tag with short-range mode enabled

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

x-error-codes.cause: Password is not a valid 8-character hex string
Target tag EPC not found in inventory

post.description:
﻿Silences one or more specific tags by EPC ID. Quieted tags don't respond during inventory rounds while the configuration is active. Other tags are unaffected.

To restore quieted tags, use `unquiet_tags`.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

tagIDs.description: List of tag EPCs (hex strings) to quiet

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

payload.message: Success: Gen2X configured. Use applyImpinjGen2X flag in start command to apply features.

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description: ﻿Restores previously silenced tags so they participate in inventory rounds again. Provide the same EPC IDs that you used when you quieted the tags.

post.description:
﻿Reads back the Gen2X configuration that's currently stored on the reader. Use this command to verify which features — such as TagProtect, FastID, TagFocus, and Tag Quieting — are active before you start the radio.

The response payload mirrors the last applied configuration. If no Gen2X configuration has been set, the response returns an empty object.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

payload.description: Current Gen2X configuration. Only previously configured feature objects are included.

fastID.description: FastID configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagProtect.description: Tag protection configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagFocus.description: TagFocus configuration (present only if previously configured)

tagQuieting.description: Tag quieting configuration (present only if previously configured)

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the payload format and retry

post.description: Starts the radio with Gen2X configuration applied. Stop the radio first before issuing this command.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

applyImpinjGen2X.description: true to apply Gen2X config when starting the radio

payload.description: Command payload containing the feature configuration

Tested example.description: Start radio with Gen2X features applied

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure

Tested example.description: Start radio with Gen2X features applied

post.description:
Stops the radio. You must stop the radio before applying any Gen2X configuration changes.

Send this command with an empty payload. Wait for the success response before you apply configuration changes and restart with `start_with_gen2x`.

command.description: MQTT command name

command_id.description: Unique identifier to correlate request with response

command.description: MQTT command name that was executed

command_id.description: Unique identifier correlating this response to the original request

response.description: Response status indicating success or failure
